payload.text:
I am building a food donation app called FoodConnect using Next.js 14, TypeScript, and Supabase. Please write the Supabase SQL Schema for this app.

It needs these specific tables with foreign key relationships:

profiles: Users (id, role [donor/volunteer], name, avatar_url, verified_status).

donations: (id, donor_id, food_items [jsonb], pickup_window_start, pickup_window_end, status [active/claimed/completed], location_lat, location_long).

claims: (id, donation_id, volunteer_id, claim_time, proof_photo_url).

Constraint: Include Row Level Security (RLS) policies so that only 'donors' can update their own donations, but 'volunteers' can view all active donations.

**Initiating Project Conception**

I'm starting by meticulously analyzing the request. My focus is on the project's core goal: developing "FoodConnect," a food donation app. I'm also examining the specified tech stack – React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and the Google GenAI SDK. This initial assessment will shape the app's architecture.


**Refining Architecture Strategy**

I'm now grappling with the user's request for a Supabase schema. The prompt specified a React SPA with Google GenAI. My strategy involves a mock backend to simulate Supabase interactions. This allows me to showcase the frontend's functionality within the existing constraints and focus on the @google/genai logic, per the system instructions. I'm prioritizing building a functional React app that simulates database behavior.


**Adapting to User Intent**

I've realized the user's intent is unclear; they mentioned a Supabase schema, yet I'm tasked with generating a React app using the Gemini API. My approach now balances both directives. I'll focus on the frontend with simulated data persistence using local state/Context. I'll *also* include a Supabase SQL schema in the code as documentation to address the user's explicit request. This ensures I provide a functional React app while accommodating their query. I'm now prioritizing getting a functional frontend built.


**Constructing the React App**

I'm now diving into the React application's structure. I've decided to prioritize a functional frontend, mimicking database interactions with `useState` and `Context`. This will allow the application to function in the browser without a real Supabase instance. My focus is on the core features: managing profiles, handling donations, and processing claims. I'll integrate the Gemini API to enhance the app with AI-powered features, such as descriptions and matching. Furthermore, I will include a "Schema Viewer" section to display the SQL schema to accommodate the user's specific request.


**Developing Core App Functionality**

I'm now focusing on integrating the features of FoodConnect. I'm prioritizing user roles, namely Donors and Volunteers, and their respective flows. I'm concentrating on the key donor tasks: profile creation, donation posting with image analysis via Gemini, and viewing donations. For volunteers, I'm working on enabling them to browse active donations, claim donations, and complete the claim process. Gemini will be used for image analysis to suggest items and expiration, while smart matching will be simulated to keep it within scope.


**Mapping App Architecture**

I'm now deep-diving into the app's structure. I've designed the files, including `metadata.json`, `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `types.ts`, `constants.ts`, `services/geminiService.ts`, `services/mockDatabase.ts`, and core components like `Layout`, `DonationCard`, `DonorDashboard`, `VolunteerDashboard`, and the requested `SchemaViewer`. I'm focusing on the service layer now. I plan to incorporate recipe ideas for volunteers. I'm focusing on simplicity, so I will prioritize visual list/grid formats over Google Maps, using Leaflet or simple lists instead. I will focus on the Gemini Service next.


**Building Features and Integration**

I'm currently working on building the "Developer Tools" modal. I'm focusing on making the SQL schema visible and easily accessible within the UI to satisfy the prompt's request. I'll utilize the mock database and I'm currently working on the "Add Donation" functionality, prioritizing the integration with the Gemini service to analyze images and suggest descriptions. I'll maintain the sustainability theme using Tailwind and a green color palette. I will make sure the UI accommodates both Donors and Volunteers with their corresponding task flows.


**Refining Final Touches**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the app. I'm focusing on robust error handling for the Gemini API interactions and ensuring the UI feels professional and polished. Also, I will render the database schema in a dedicated component. I will integrate the Gemini API as requested. I've opted for direct Base64 handling of images. I am now focused on a responsive, easy-to-use user interface.


**Prioritizing Model Integration**

I've selected the Gemini models: `gemini-2.5-flash-image` for image analysis and `gemini-3-flash-preview` for text tasks, to optimize for the project's requirements. I'm focusing on their implementation. Also, I am now working on the specific components of the UI and how the Gemini API will integrate into them.

entries.description: Root HTML file with Tailwind CSS.
Entry point for the React application.
Service for interacting with Google Gemini API.
Component to display the requested SQL schema.
Main application component with state management and routing logic.

payload.text: Refine the 'Donate' form to include a more intuitive quantity selector (e.g., slider or number input with steppers) and a clearer pickup window selection, potentially using a time range picker.

**Refining Donate Form**

I'm currently focused on improving the donation form in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've started exploring more intuitive options for the quantity selector, considering a slider or number input with steppers for a better user experience. Additionally, I'm researching a clearer pickup window selection, perhaps using a time range picker to streamline the donation process.


**Evaluating Time Window Options**

I'm now diving into the time window selection component within the donation form in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I'm comparing time range pickers to a custom date and time input solution. My goal is to make the process as seamless as possible for donors, so they can easily communicate their availability. I will refine the best option to fit the project's user interface.


**Improving Time Input Fields**

I'm now revisiting the pickup window, specifically the time input fields within `pages/Donate.tsx`. While the existing setup uses native time inputs, I can improve them. I plan to refine the interface by enhancing the appearance of the quantity selector with steppers, and by improving the styling of the two native `time` inputs, with the aim of creating a more intuitive and visually appealing experience.


**Implementing UI Enhancements**

I'm now implementing the UI improvements for `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've redesigned the quantity selector with a centered number input, flanking minus/plus buttons, and a slider. The pickup window now features "quick select" chips for common timeframes along with enhanced start/end time inputs, visually connected to represent the duration. I'm focusing on ensuring a clean, intuitive design.


**Refining Time Component**

I'm now integrating "quick select" chips for the time window in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added options like 1h, 2h, and 4h, which automatically set the start and end times. I'm focusing on ensuring these quick selections are intuitive and work smoothly. I am now working on error handling.


**Finalizing Donation Form Logic**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the donation form logic within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added error handling to the time selection component, ensuring that the end time does not occur before the start time. Additionally, I've incorporated helper functions to refine the quantity stepper functionality. The adjustments now stay within range, as well as handle the end-of-day cases.


**Optimizing UI Design**

I'm now refining the quantity selector in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I'm focusing on the input field within its design, specifically styling the number input to create a more integrated and aesthetically pleasing appearance within the broader UI. This includes ensuring consistent font sizes, spacing, and overall visual balance.


**Enhancing Slider Component**

I'm now revising the slider component in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added labels at the beginning, middle, and end of the slider's range to guide users in their donation selections. These labels specify kilograms and help illustrate what the slider represents. I'm focusing on ensuring it's clear and the values are obvious.


**Improving Time UI**

I'm now revisiting the "quick select" chips in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added button functionality. I will also be working on refining the appearance and functionality of these quick-select options, ensuring they're both intuitive to interact with and clearly represent the available donation time slots. I'm focusing on user clarity.


**Refining Time Input Styling**

I'm now focusing on the visual design of the time input fields within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I'm going to refine the padding, and I'll focus on the border styles of the native `time` input fields to improve their overall appearance. My aim is to make the input fields more visually appealing and consistent with the rest of the donation form's UI.


**Styling Time Inputs**

I'm now implementing styling changes to the time input fields in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I will adjust the padding and refine the border styles to improve their visual appearance and ensure consistency within the UI. I want the inputs to be more integrated.


**Revising Time Logic**

I'm now integrating the logic to handle time window selections within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've decided to label the "Afternoon Slot" in JSX as "+4 Hours" to ensure that the user interface matches the underlying relative time logic. I also added necessary imports like `React`, `useState`, `useRef`, and several icons, as I'm replacing the entire file's content. I'm focusing on simplicity.


**Implementing Form Input Fields**

I'm now integrating new form input fields in `pages/Donate.tsx`, including number input with steppers, represented as plus/minus buttons with a middle input field. I am also making sure the layout is clean. I am also ensuring that the input features the requested design.


**Managing Form State**

I'm now focusing on managing the form's state within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've set up state variables for editing mode, success indicators, and an edit prompt. I've also initialized state for the title, selected tags, quantity, start and end times, and file handling using `useState` and `useRef`. My focus is on ensuring data persistence and smooth user interactions.


**Integrating Image Editing**

I'm now integrating the image editing functionality within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I'm working on the `handleEditImage` function. I've set up state variables for editing mode, as well as an edit prompt. I've also incorporated the logic for making an API call to edit the image, along with error handling to provide feedback to the user. I'm focusing on user experience.


**Refining Tag Selection**

I'm now implementing the tag selection feature within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've created the `handleTagClick` function, which toggles tag selection by updating the `selectedTags` state. This new logic allows tags to be added or removed from the list of selected tags. I'm focusing on ensuring responsiveness.



**Implementing Time Logic**

I'm now integrating logic within `pages/Donate.tsx` to handle specific edge cases for time window selection. I'm focusing on ensuring the end time doesn't fall before the start time and handles end-of-day cases gracefully by setting end time to 23:59. I am updating my submission handlers. I'm focusing on user-friendly time controls.


**Updating Submission Handler**

I'm now focusing on the form's submission handler in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I'm working to display updated submission messaging, depending on whether it's an update or new donation. I'm focusing on user feedback after the submission. I will also be revising the messaging of the submission confirmation.


**Refining Image Upload**

I'm now focusing on refining the image upload component in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added a file selection handler and incorporated an accept attribute to ensure users are uploading valid file types. I'm focusing on user feedback by adding text, and making an upload box that is visually consistent.


**Integrating Image Deletion**

I'm now integrating image deletion functionality into the upload component within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I have added a button with an "X" icon to the image preview. When clicked, it will clear the selected file, remove the preview, and reset associated form fields like title and tags. I'm prioritizing quick actions.


**Implementing Magic Edit Bar**

I'm now integrating the "Magic Edit Bar" within the image upload component in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added a text input field, enabling users to enter prompts for image enhancements. I am also working on the `handleEditImage` functionality, which triggers the AI-driven image editing process. I am making it feel seamless.


**Finalizing AI Integration**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the AI-driven image editing feature in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I am focusing on the "Apply" button's state, to disable it when `isEditing`, and re-enable after the image is fully generated. I am also making sure the text is accurate. I'm focusing on user control and smooth transitions.


**Refining Tag Selection Function**

I'm now refining the `toggleTag` function in `pages/Donate.tsx`. I'm working to make the tag selection more intuitive by adding or removing tags from the `selectedTags` state. This functionality directly impacts the overall user experience. I'm focusing on responsiveness.



**Improving Quantity Component**

I'm now revising the quantity selector in `pages/Donate.tsx`, with focus on the visual aspects. I'm restyling the number input for a consistent appearance, ensuring it feels more integrated into the form. The UI is being refined for a seamless, more pleasant, user experience.


**Adjusting Quantity Display**

I'm now integrating a numeric display with units and stepper controls within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I am aiming for better visual clarity with text next to the slider, along with buttons. I also added styling and responsive functionality. The aim is better visual feedback.


**Enhancing UI Elements**

I'm now implementing the numeric display within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added a number input, coupled with stepper controls and units, for a more user-friendly and visually clear quantity selector. This improves visual clarity and overall responsiveness.


**Refining Time Button Styling**

I'm now implementing the time quick select buttons within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added button styling. I'm focusing on their functionality to ensure seamless time slot selection. The buttons are clear and consistent.



**Styling Time Inputs**

I'm now styling the time input fields within `pages/Donate.tsx`. I've added a visual connection with an arrow icon. I am refining the padding, and border styles. The goal is to make these input fields more aesthetically consistent with the rest of the form's design.

entries.description: Refined the Donate form with an intuitive quantity stepper/slider and improved pickup window selection with quick-select options.

payload.text: on the post donation page the background of the food title text box should be white not black so the text will be visible

**Analyzing the Donation Page**

Okay, I've started digging into the `Donate.tsx` code. The key issue appears to be a visual glitch on the "Post Donation" page, specifically with the "Food Title" input field. The background seems to be rendering incorrectly, making the text hard to read. I'm focusing on that section of the component.


**Investigating the UI Elements**

I'm now zeroing in on the UI component's styling. The "Food Title" input field seems to be inheriting a default background, or maybe there's an issue in the cascade. Specifically, the component lacks an explicit background color definition in its class string, leading me to suspect that a default or inherited style is causing the display issue.


**Clarifying the Styling Inheritance**

I'm now thinking about the styling inheritance for the "Food Title" input. I missed something in the previous turn, so I'm re-analyzing the `Donate.tsx` file for the styles. The problem likely stems from either a missing `bg-white` class or some other dark default style being applied, which might be why the input's background is not appearing as expected, especially if the parent container doesn't explicitly define a light background. My focus is on ensuring the component receives an explicit `bg-white` class, or is styled so that the background remains transparent and can pick up the white background from its surroundings.


**Pinpointing the Root Cause**

I've circled back to the `Donate.tsx` file, meticulously examining the `className` attribute of the input field. I've realized I've overlooked the missing `bg-white` class, which is crucial since the global styles set a light background color for the body. This oversight leads to the "Food Title" input displaying a default background (possibly dark due to the user agent styles) and making the text difficult to see because the default text color is dark. Also, I think there is low contrast between the text and background, probably making the text hard to read.


**Refining the Input Styles**

I've been reviewing the `Donate.tsx` file again, focusing on the `className` attribute of the "Food Title" input. It looks like the issue is a missing background color specification, which allows a darker default to take hold, resulting in low contrast. I'm going to add the `bg-white` and `text-stone-900` classes to the component's styling. These classes should ensure a white background and dark text, improving readability and addressing the reported visibility issue on the "Post Donation" page. I'm confident that this modification will resolve the problem.


**Confirming Classname Updates**

I'm now completely confident that adding `bg-white text-stone-900` to the `className` attribute of the "Food Title" input will fix the visibility problem. I also re-checked the edit prompt input inside the image section and confirmed that the `bg-transparent` class there is appropriate given its white container. I also think I should double-check the quantity input's style.


**Verifying Component Styles**

I've just been looking at the quantity input field, and it seems the transparent background class is appropriate, especially given its container's background. I'm also reviewing the time input fields within the `Donate.tsx` file, and confirmed they have the correct `bg-white` class for a white background. It looks like the title input was the sole component missing these style specifications. I'm confident in my updates, and will proceed with the proposed change.


**Implementing the CSS Update**

I'm now implementing the `bg-white text-stone-900` classes to the title input, as well as keeping `bg-transparent` in the edit input. Also, I've confirmed that the quantity field's transparent style is also correct. I believe these updates will resolve the previously observed readability issues, so I'm pushing these changes through.


**Reviewing State Variables**

I'm now diving into the state variables within `Donate.tsx`, ensuring everything is correctly initialized and managed. I'm taking another look at the `useState` hook calls for `startT`, `endT`, and all the other variables to confirm the components are correctly initializing these variables. I want to make sure I did not miss anything when reviewing the variables, and confirming their current state.



**Reviewing Asynchronous Operations**

I'm now closely examining the asynchronous operations in `Donate.tsx`, specifically the image analysis and editing functions. I'm focusing on the `try...catch...finally` blocks within `handleImageUpload` and `handleEditImage`. I'm verifying the correct handling of states like `isAnalyzing` and `isEditing`, and also ensuring that I'm setting the state variables like `title`, `selectedTags`, and `quantity` correctly after successful operations. I also want to double check the error handling, which I have not updated.


**Reviewing Function State Management**

I'm now investigating the state management within the functions `handleTags`, `adjustQuantity`, and `setQuickTime` to ensure everything functions as I intended. I'm focusing specifically on how the state updates affect the components' behavior and ensuring that all calculations are correct, especially around time and quantity. I also have to investigate how I can implement error handling.



**Examining Form Submission Logic**

I'm now analyzing the form's `handleSubmit` function and the construction of the `newDonation` object. I'm making sure all relevant data is included: title, weight, tags, pickup window, and image URL. Also, I need to ensure the `onSave` function receives this data correctly. I'm also ensuring the correct states and values are updated and managed after submission.



**Verifying Post-Submit States**

I'm focusing now on confirming the post-submission state changes and notifications within the `Donate.tsx` component, I need to confirm the text after a successful submission. I'm reviewing the conditional rendering within the component, specifically the text related to saved changes and notifications. I'm making sure the correct messages are displayed depending on `initialData`.


**Evaluating the Image Upload**

I'm now deeply involved in the image upload section, scrutinizing the conditional rendering based on `previewUrl`. I am evaluating the state change and interactions between `fileInputRef`, `handleFileSelect`, and the image display logic. Specifically, I'm verifying the display logic when previewUrl is present or absent.



**Refining Error Handling**

I'm now zeroing in on error handling within `Donate.tsx`. I'm going through the `try...catch` blocks within the image upload functions and the form submission, and am adding clearer error messages and better state management for error scenarios. I want to improve feedback to the user on image loading.


**Improving Error Handling**

I'm now focusing on strengthening the error management in `Donate.tsx`, particularly within the asynchronous image operations and form submission. I'm adding more specific error messages and improving state updates for error handling. I want to provide better user feedback for image loading, image editing, and potential form submission problems.


**Refactoring the UI Components**

I'm now overhauling the UI components, and I am specifically evaluating button and form elements. I'm taking another look at the image editing and apply functionality to ensure they have the proper styling. Also, I'm refactoring the styling of form elements to ensure consistent design.



**Optimizing UI Element Styles**

I'm now zeroing in on the tag component and have confirmed that the styles are appropriately rendered given their current styling configuration. I am going to investigate the quantity styling and update the configuration if necessary.


**Refining Quantity Styling**

I am now focusing on the quantity input field styling within `Donate.tsx`. I have reviewed the tag component's styling and verified it renders as intended. The quantity input's styling looks good. I'll make sure the `adjustQuantity` function is working properly.



**Validating Quantity Input**

I am now validating the quantity input styles and logic. The display looks correct, and the `adjustQuantity` function seems to work. I'm double-checking the visual consistency of the number display with the other inputs. I am now looking over the quantity input field, specifically verifying the range input functionality.


**Validating Time Selection**

I'm now focusing on the time selection components, specifically the quick select buttons. I'm verifying the functionality of the `setQuickTime` function and its impact on the `startT` and `endT` states. I'll make sure that these updates are working as expected.


**Validating Time Selection Logic**

I'm now deep-diving into the quick time selection buttons. I am checking the current implementation of the `setQuickTime` function, and how it affects the `startT` and `endT` states. I'm confirming these values are updated correctly and reflect the selected time window.


**Validating Time Input Fields**

I'm now checking the input fields for start and end times, to confirm they render correctly. I'm verifying their states with the quick select buttons. Also, I'm verifying the input's current values.

I have updated `pages/Donate.tsx` to explicitly add `bg-white` and `text-stone-900` to the food title input field. This ensures high readability and overrides any browser defaults or dark mode inheritance that may have caused the black background issue.

entries.description: Added bg-white and text-stone-900 to the title input to ensure readability.
